title: Reading Comprehension
objective: Students will explore reading comprehension concepts including Story Sequence and Central Message, Non-Fiction Text Features, Characters' Viewpoints and Responses and more.

steps.content: Let's discover the amazing world of Story Sequence and Central Message!

Discuss the sequence of events in narrative texts and how items of information are related in non-fiction, retelling stories including key details and demonstrating understanding of central message or lesson
Time to try this out: Retell a story in correct sequence with beginning, middle, and end
After you finishes a chapter book, can they explain the beginning, middle, and end in their own words — and describe what the story was really about at its heart?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Story Sequence and Central Message. Retell a story in correct sequence with beginning, middle, and end Identify the central message or lesson of a story
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Non-Fiction Text Features!
Recognise different non-fiction text structures and features (headings, contents, glossary, index), understanding how texts are organised to present information
Let me show you something cool. Use a contents page to find information on a specific topic in a non-fiction book
When you is using a non-fiction book for a school project, do they know how to use the headings, contents page, and index to find what they need quickly?

Great job! Today we learned about Non-Fiction Text Features. Use a contents page to find information on a specific topic in a non-fiction book Identify the purpose of headings, glossary, and index in an information text
Let's discover the amazing world of Characters' Viewpoints and Responses!

Identify and compare characters' points of view, recognise who is narrating a story, describe how characters respond to events and challenges, and compare characters' experiences across different stories or versions of the same story
Time to try this out: Identify who is telling a story and explain how you know (e.g. 'The wolf is telling the story because he says I')
When you reads a story told from one character's point of view, can they think about how a different character in the same story might have seen events differently?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Characters' Viewpoints and Responses: Identify who is telling a story and explain how you know (e.g. 'The wolf is telling the story because he says I') Describe how a character responds to a problem or challenge using evidence from the text (e.g. 'When the bridge broke, she decided to swim across')
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Retelling Stories with Structure!

Retell stories including key details in sequence, describe characters, settings, and major events using evidence from the text, and describe the overall structure of a story (beginning, middle, ending)
Time to try this out: Retell a familiar story in order, including at least three key details from beginning, middle, and end
After reading a story, can you describe the main character — including what kind of person they are, what they wanted, and how they felt at the end — using details from the book?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Retelling Stories with Structure. Retell a familiar story in order, including at least three key details from beginning, middle, and end Describe a character's appearance, actions, or personality using details from the text
Let's explore Expressive and Sensory Language! Have you ever wondered about this?
Recognise recurring literary language in stories and poetry, identify words and phrases that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses, and discuss favourite words and phrases
Time to try this out: Spot recurring story language such as 'Once upon a time' and 'happily ever after'
When you reads a poem or a story with vivid language, can they pick out a phrase they really liked — and tell you why it works well or how it made them feel?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Expressive and Sensory Language. Spot recurring story language such as 'Once upon a time' and 'happily ever after' Identify sensory words and phrases in a poem: 'the icy wind howled'
Let's explore Main Topic & Key Details! Have you ever wondered about this?
Identify the main topic of a multi-paragraph informational text, determine the focus of specific paragraphs, and explain how key details support the main idea
Time to try this out: Identify the main topic of a multi-paragraph informational text and state the focus of individual paragraphs
When you reads a multi-page article — like a school text about a historical event — can they tell you the main idea and then explain what each section was specifically about?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Main Topic & Key Details. Identify the main topic of a multi-paragraph informational text and state the focus of individual paragraphs Describe how specific details and facts in the text support the main idea
